# The Cartographer's Heir

## High-Concept Logline
A nineteenth-century Bukharan map-maker inherits her father's atlas of contested
borders the day Tsarist surveyors arrive — every map she authenticates costs her
a witness who can verify the next.

## Audience Size & Evidence
Estimated audience: 1,800,000,000 (source: [Pew Research](https://www.pewresearch.org/religion/2015/04/02/muslims/))
Countries: SA (400M), ID (229M), PK (200M), BD (153M), IN (140M), NG (99M) [6 of 3 or more required]
Trend: rising (source: [Pew 2017 Global Muslim Population Projections](https://www.pewresearch.org/religion/2017/11/29/globally-the-median-age-of-muslims-is-7-years-younger-than-the-median-age-of-non-muslims/))

## JTBD
JTBD-7 / Diaspora Witnesses Defending Inherited Geography
Deprivation: Communities erased from official cartography; oral testimony as the
only ledger; the daughter as the last reader of her father's marginalia.

## Asset
**Bukharan map-makers' guild, 1868** (historical_event)
Emotional charge: Inheritance as conscription; the weight of a profession that
costs witnesses; the loneliness of certifying what an empire intends to deny.

## TRIZ Contradiction
**Contradiction 7: Reliability vs. Adaptability**
North pole: Cartographic authentication requires named, living witnesses — any
shortcut hands the empire a counter-claim.
South pole: Survival requires anonymizing those same witnesses — naming them
costs them, and the atlas is unfinished without their evidence.

| STC | Genre 7: Institutionalized |
| Truby | Archetype 2: Moral Argument |

## Key Roles
**Protagonist:** Zarina ibn-Khalid — guild authentication chief; goal: complete
her father's atlas before the surveyors close every contested ledger
**Antagonist:** Captain Volkov — Tsarist cartographer; same goal (definitive
atlas), opposite method (state survey instruments, not testimony)
**Ally:** Old Tashkenti scribe Yusuf — keeper of the unsigned witness lists
**Mentor:** null

## Closing Image
A lit oil-lamp on the unfinished atlas; outside, Volkov's surveyors fold their
brass theodolites and ride into snow that already mismatches their map.
